course_description:
This course will continue to build on the midwifery foundational skills learned in Semester 1 with a focus on the principles of medication administration in the mother and neonate. Safety in drug administration as well as observational techniques for the mother and baby required when drugs are utilised will be learned. Students will develop a comprehensive understanding of the principles and mechanisms of normal labour and birth and conduct in practice care and support of a woman through a normal labour and birth. Non-pharmacological methods of pain relief in pregnancy, labour and birth are also considered. In the clinical practice aspect of this course, the attainment of Midwifery Practice Experiences (MPEs) are also required.

This course will provide students with ongoing opportunities to develop their clinical practice skills. The focus of the semester will surround medication administration and the safe practice when performing this task. Students will also build upon their role in supporting the woman during labour and birth.

Throughout the semester, clinical learning experiences will be planned to integrate with the theoretical concepts covered in the companion courses. These experiences will occur throughout the semester at your clinical placement site. Students will be rostered to attend 120 hours, either morning, afternoon or night duty shifts for eight (8) hours duration, and will complete fifteen (15) shifts during the semester. Students must be aware that when they are attending clinical placement, they are there as representatives of the School of Nursing, Midwifery and Social Work of the University of Queensland, therefore, professional deportment and professional behaviour is essential. In addition to their clinical placement, students will have planned clinical based learning (CBL) sessions in the laboratories with a Lecturer. In these sessions, they will learn specific skills that relate to the course, which they may then implement under supervision in their clinical placement.

Throughout the semester students are required to submit evidence of progress during placement according to assessment and learning activity requirements as outlined in Sections 6 and 8. If further evidence is required, students will be advised accordingly. Students who do not achieve/maintain clinical performance commensurate with learning and experience at any point in the semester may be placed on a practice learning plan by the Course Coordinator in consultation with the Practice Education Manager (Midwifery) / Clinical placement facilitator for ongoing support with the development of skills and clinical performance. If fitness to practice and safety concerns are raised, students may be withdrawn from the clinical placement at the discretion of the Practice Education Manager (Midwifery) / Program Lead, and in consultation with the Head of School.

course_aims: The course aims to provide students with a foundation of midwifery skills through clinical practice gained in different midwifery settings. This course will continue to emphasise to students the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Australia (NMBA) Midwife Standards of Practice (2018) and how these standards provide a framework for the care of women and babies.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: Explore the role of the midwife in providing woman centred inter-professional care including those requiring drug therapy during pregnancy, birth, and the postnatal period.

Exemplify and utilise available technologies to fulfil clinical requirements for attainment of midwifery practice and continuity of care experiences through recruitment, partnership, and development of effective relationships with women and the healthcare team.

Explain the legal and professional responsibilities, and the frameworks for safe midwifery practice including knowledge of scope of practice and the need to accept accountability and responsibility for safe drug administration.

Explain and demonstrate beginning level midwifery practice skills including monitoring and care of the woman and her baby's health, wellbeing, and responses to implemented care including administration of drugs in pregnancy, labour and birth, and the postpartum period.

Interpret how personal beliefs and the philosophy of woman centred care reflect continued enhancement of ethical decision making in the context of physical, psychological, and culturally responsive midwifery practice.

assessment_details.title: Clinical Placement Preparation and Development
assessment_details.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Participation/ Student contribution, Placement, Practical/ Demonstration, Portfolio, Quiz, Reflection
assessment_details.due_date: Med+Safe to be completed in Week 3. Completion of the Skill and Mid-point AMSAT will occur at approximately halfway (60 hours) of the student's placement. This includes in-semester and out of semester placements. All requirements must be submitted and met to complete this assessment which contributes to passing the course.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment task comprises of three (3) parts which will examine and extend your preparedness for clinical placement and development in the first half of the semester. Part 1 – Medication safety This assessment task requires the student to undertake an online assessment on medication safety as well as completion of a drug calculations assessment. Students must receive a 100% pass mark to be able to pass this assessment. Further instructions can be found in the assessment task and via the BlackBoard site . Part 2 - Skill Assessment This assessment requires demonstration to perform a range of skills required for competent and safe midwifery practice. The aim of this task is to actively seek feedback about your practice, and evaluate your own performance to plan for improvements. Part 3 - Demonstrated capability in clinical practice commensurate with the MIDW1105 Course Learning Objectives evidenced by completion and submission of the Australian Midwifery Standards Assessment Tool (AMSAT) The Mid-point AMSAT must be completed in Myprogress ePAD and signed by your Practice Educator/ Clinical Facilitator OR Preceptor Midwife. Completion due date is mid placement (approx. 60 hours of the student's placement) - complete Standards 1, 2, and 3 only For all parts, please access the task description in the blackboard site for more information. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students are required to upload their completed Med+Safe certificates to the companion blackboard site under Assessments.
Students are to complete their Skills and Mid-point AMSAT via their ePAD.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessment_details.category: Notebook/ Logbook, Participation/ Student contribution, Placement, Practical/ Demonstration, Portfolio, Reflection
assessment_details.due_date: The due date for this assessment is based upon your clinical placement start date with the assessment being due by the Friday of your final week of placement. This assessment requires the completion of Skills, Final AMSAT, and MPE and CoCE. This includes in-semester and out of semester placements. All requirements must be submitted and met in order to complete this assessment which contributes to passing the course.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment requires you to demonstrate successful completion of clinical placement. There are three (3) parts to this assessment: Part 1 - Skill Assessment This assessment requires demonstration to perform a range of skills required for competent and safe midwifery practice. The aim of this task is to actively seek feedback about your practice, and evaluate your own performance to plan for improvements. Part 2 - Demonstrated capability in clinical practice commensurate with the MIDW1105 Course Learning Objectives evidenced by completion and submission of the Australian Midwifery Standards Assessment Tool (AMSAT) The Final AMSAT must be completed in Myprogress ePAD and signed by your Practice Educator/ Clinical Facilitator OR Preceptor Midwife. Completion due date is at the end of your placement (around 120 hours) - complete Standards 1, 2, and 3 only Part 3 - Completion and upload of required Midwifery Practice Experiences (MPE) including Continuity of Care Experiences (CoCE). This assessment requires the student to upload at least 30 MPE and recruit at least one (1) CoCE. For all parts, please access the task description in the blackboard site for more information. Read full task description Hide full task description

Students are to complete their MPE and CoCE requirements via their ePAD.

learning_activities.topic: Clinical Based Learning Workshops (CBL) Clinical skill development in a simulated learning environment. ATTENDANCE IS COMPULSORY . Further details of each workshop can be found in Clinical Based learning on the companion Blackboard course. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 13
learning_activities.topic: MIDW1105 Clinical Midwifery Practice 2 Clinical practice attendance at allocated hospital. Completion of all fifteen (15) clinical practice shifts or 120 hours of clinical practice is COMPULSORY . Students will be complete a placement as in semester or out of semester placement, as allocated by the Practice Education Team. Learning outcomes: L03
Course Introduction and Requirements Identification of the course requirements, assessments, and ongoing requirements for the CoCE and MPE. Learning outcomes: L02, L03

Employability Lecture and Myprogress ePAD Career development workshop on understanding your professional self. Refresher on the ePortfolio platform Myprogress ePAD which will be used to record all MPEs and CoCEs completed throughout their program. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Working with Children Policy Fitness to Practise Policy Vaccinations and Immunisation Procedure Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Course guidelines Students must comply with the Clinical Placement Shift and Rostering Guidelines, Mandatory preparation requirements and Scope of Practice documents. Refer to the Clinical Placements Tab in the companion Blackboard site for details. Learning activities
